title: Within-lap ranking auxiliary model
summary: At a given race lap, only a small subset of drivers are realistic next-lap pit candidates.
rationale: At a given race lap, only a small subset of drivers are realistic next-lap pit candidates. Learning-to-rank objectives are designed for exactly this grouped 'who is most likely now' problem and may complement binary AUC models.
implementation_hint: Create groups by `Year,Race,LapNumber`; train a CatBoost or LightGBM ranker to rank drivers by pit likelihood within each lap, then blend its scores with the binary model. Test top-heavy query objectives and use group-aware folds only.
expected_effect: Often improves ordering among hard borderline cases even when standalone calibration is weaker.
risk: Ranking loss optimizes intra-lap ordering, not absolute probability, so it may need careful blending and calibration.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
